page: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-16 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 16 vCPUs and 64 GB of system memory. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor using the x86_64 architecture, this server features 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU allocation is dedicated, providing consistent processing resources. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache. It supports hardware features such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX), AES cryptography, and SHA extensions, though it lacks AVX-512 capabilities. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

Benchmark evaluations position the e2-standard-16 predominantly in the average tier for multi-core and memory operations. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is a notable exception, scoring 2678.78, which ranks in the top 25% of tested hardware. Multi-core CPU performance registers at 21462.05, placing it in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests show average throughput, with read-only small block cached speeds at 1016.71 GB/sec and large block uncached speeds at 113.16 GB/sec. Database operations via Passmark score 6458.81, and Redis SET operations achieve 10.37 million operations per second, both aligning with mid-range performance expectations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the e2-standard-16 offers a balanced resource density of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU, making it a cost-effective option for workloads that require moderate, steady-state compute and memory without the premium cost of specialized hardware. However, tradeoffs include the absence of local NVMe storage and the lack of AVX-512 instructions, which limits its utility for advanced vector math or high-throughput cryptography. This instance is suitable for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, text processing, and small-scale database operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-16 is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It provides 8 physical cores with dedicated CPU allocation, 16 MB of L3 cache, and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the middle 50% for most multi-core and memory operations, though its single-core stress-ng CPU performance ranks in the top 25% at 2678.78. Memory bandwidth reaches 113.16 GB/sec for large block reads, and Redis SET operations perform at 10.37 million operations per second. Lacking AVX-512 support, this instance represents a balanced, cost-efficient option for steady-state workloads. It is suited for general-purpose applications, including static web serving, software compilation, and standard database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-16 is a general-purpose instance with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B12 CPU, delivering balanced mid-range performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-16 is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and dedicated AMD EPYC 7B12 processing cores.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, steady multi-core performance, and a standard memory-to-vCPU ratio.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 64 GB RAM powered by an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor
Dedicated CPU allocation with 8 physical cores and 2 threads per core
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25%
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance profiles
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations
